source: ICH Q3C(R9) Guideline for Residual Solvents

class1.name: Class 1 - Solvents to Be Avoided
class1.description: Known human carcinogens, strongly suspected carcinogens, and environmental hazards
class1.rule: Should not be employed in manufacture unless use is unavoidable with benefit/risk justification

class2.name: Class 2 - Solvents to Be Limited
class2.description: Non-genotoxic animal carcinogens or possible causative agents of other irreversible toxicity such as neurotoxicity or teratogenicity
class2.rule: Should be limited in pharmaceutical products; PDE given to nearest 0.1 mg/day, concentrations to nearest 10 ppm

solvents.name: Xylene
solvents.note: usually 60% m-xylene, 14% p-xylene, 9% o-xylene with 17% ethyl benzene

class3.name: Class 3 - Solvents with Low Toxic Potential
class3.description: Low toxic potential to man; no long-term toxicity or carcinogenicity studies for many, but negative in genotoxicity studies
class3.rule: Amounts of 50 mg/day or less (5000 ppm or 0.5% under Option 1) acceptable without justification

unlisted.name: Unlisted Solvents
unlisted.description: Solvents not included in Class 1, 2, or 3
unlisted.rule: Manufacturer should supply justification for residual levels based on toxicological data

acceptable_supplier_statements: Only Class 3 solvents are likely to be present. Loss on drying is less than 0.5%.
Only Class 2 solvents X, Y, ... are likely to be present. All are below the Option 1 limit.
Only Class 2 solvents X, Y, ... and Class 3 solvents are likely to be present. Residual Class 2 solvents are below the Option 1 limit and residual Class 3 solvents are below 0.5%.

testing_requirements.class1: If likely to be present, must be identified and quantified
testing_requirements.class2_class3_above_limit: If present at greater than Option 1 limits (Class 2) or 0.5% (Class 3), must be identified and quantified
testing_requirements.likely_to_be_present_definition: Solvent used in final manufacturing step AND solvents used in earlier steps not removed consistently by validated process

option1.name: Option 1 - Use concentration limits from Table 2
option1.description: Concentration limits in ppm stated in Table 2 can be used. Calculated using assumed daily dose of 10 g.

option2.name: Option 2 - Use PDE from Table 2
option2.description: PDE in terms of mg/day as stated in Table 2 can be used with known maximum daily dose to calculate allowed concentration in drug substance.

class3_control.name: Class 3 Solvent Control
methods: GC test for individual Class 3 solvents
class3_control.note: LOD is acceptable control method for Class 3 solvents when used in final isolation step
